description: Add Anthropic Claude (Haiku) as primary post-processor via SSH→Oracle (voice_input-5ey). Gemini Vertex AI has become unreliable. Mirror the existing vertex_proxy.py + process_with_vertex_ai/process_with_gemini_merge pattern. Add claude-fix (single ASR) and claude-merge (dual ASR) presets. Keep existing gemini-* and haiku-fix presets intact. Auto-migrate users from gemini-merge → claude-merge in DB. OpenRouter remains the final fallback. Anthropic API key already deployed at oracle-cloud:~/.config/claude.secret.

userStories.title: Create anthropic_proxy.py on Oracle Cloud
userStories.description: Create a self-contained anthropic_proxy.py script (parallel to vertex_proxy.py) that reads JSON from stdin, calls Anthropic API via the anthropic SDK, writes text to stdout. Place at /home/dev/code/voice_input/anthropic_proxy.py in this repo (will be deployed to Oracle via scp). Stdin JSON fields: system_prompt, user_input, model (default 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'), max_tokens (default 1024). Reads API key from ~/.config/claude.secret. Exit 0 on success (stdout=text), exit 1 on failure (stderr=error). Implements --help and --test flags like vertex_proxy.py. Uses anthropic SDK: client.messages.create(model=..., max_tokens=..., system=..., messages=[{role:'user', content:...}]).
acceptanceCriteria: File /home/dev/code/voice_input/anthropic_proxy.py exists at repo root
Reads JSON from stdin with fields: system_prompt, user_input, model, max_tokens
Calls Anthropic API using anthropic SDK Messages API
Reads API key from ~/.config/claude.secret (Path expansion)
On API success: prints response.content[0].text.strip() to stdout, exits 0
On any error: prints error to stderr, exits 1
Supports --help flag with usage documentation
Supports --test flag that verifies SDK import + API key file readable
Self-contained: no imports from voice_input project (must run standalone on Oracle)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add process_with_anthropic() and process_with_anthropic_merge() to post_processor_configs.py
userStories.description: Add two new functions to post_processor_configs.py that mirror process_with_vertex_ai() and process_with_gemini_merge() but call anthropic_proxy.py via SSH. Same SSH command shape as vertex: ssh oracle-cloud python3 ~/anthropic_proxy.py. Same JSON stdin payload format with max_tokens computed via min(8192, max(512, len(user_input))). Reuse _run_vertex_proxy() — it is already proxy-script-agnostic (takes cmd list); just pass a different cmd. Reuse OpenRouter fallback identical to existing vertex_ai functions. Hallucination guard (2x) and question guard same as existing.
acceptanceCriteria: process_with_anthropic(text, config, glossary_ctx='') function added
process_with_anthropic_merge(primary_text, secondary_text, config, glossary_ctx='') function added
SSH cmd is: ['ssh', '-o', 'ConnectTimeout=5', config['ssh_host'], 'python3', config['proxy_script']]
JSON stdin includes system_prompt, user_input, model, max_tokens (NOT max_output_tokens — Anthropic SDK uses max_tokens)
max_tokens formula: min(8192, max(512, len(user_input)))
min_text_len guard works same as existing (short text returns primary)
OpenRouter fallback called on failure (returncode != 0 or timeout), with same notify text on both-failed
Hallucination guard (output > 2x input → return original) preserved
Question guard (input has ？ but output doesn't → return original) preserved
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add claude-fix and claude-merge presets to post_processor_presets.py
userStories.description: Add two new presets parallel to gemini-fix and gemini-merge. claude-fix uses framework 'anthropic'. claude-merge uses framework 'anthropic-merge'. Both point to ssh_host='oracle-cloud', proxy_script='~/anthropic_proxy.py', model='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'. Reuse existing prompts: prompts/gemini-fix-system.txt for claude-fix, prompts/gemini-merge-system.txt for claude-merge (same task, different LLM — prompts are LLM-agnostic). Change DEFAULT_POST_PROCESSOR from 'gemini-merge' to 'claude-merge'. Do NOT delete or modify existing gemini-* or haiku-fix presets — they stay as alternatives.
acceptanceCriteria: POST_PROCESSOR_PRESETS contains 'claude-fix' entry with framework='anthropic'
POST_PROCESSOR_PRESETS contains 'claude-merge' entry with framework='anthropic-merge'
Both presets set ssh_host='oracle-cloud', proxy_script='~/anthropic_proxy.py'
Both presets set model='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', timeout=15, min_text_len=15, vocab_min_count=3
claude-fix uses system_prompt_file='prompts/gemini-fix-system.txt' and user_prompt_template_file='prompts/haiku-fix-user.txt'
claude-merge uses system_prompt_file='prompts/gemini-merge-system.txt' (no user template — merge format is built in code)
DEFAULT_POST_PROCESSOR == 'claude-merge'
All existing presets (none, chinese-text-correction, qwen3-0.6b, minicpm4-0.5b, haiku-fix, haiku-expand, gemini-fix, gemini-merge) remain unchanged
Tests pass

userStories.title: Dispatch anthropic/anthropic-merge frameworks in voice_input.py _post_process()
userStories.description: Add framework dispatching for 'anthropic' (single ASR → process_with_anthropic) and 'anthropic-merge' (dual ASR → process_with_anthropic_merge) in ASRDaemon._post_process(). Mirror exactly how 'vertex-ai' and 'vertex-ai-merge' frameworks are dispatched — they should produce identical control flow. Anthropic does not need a secondary model load (faster-whisper) for the single fix variant; the merge variant needs secondary_text from self._last_secondary_text, same as vertex-ai-merge.
acceptanceCriteria: _post_process() handles framework='anthropic' by calling process_with_anthropic(text, config, glossary_ctx)
_post_process() handles framework='anthropic-merge' by calling process_with_anthropic_merge(primary, secondary, config, glossary_ctx)
load_post_processor() handles 'anthropic-merge' framework by loading secondary model (faster-whisper), same as 'vertex-ai-merge'
load_post_processor() handles 'anthropic' framework without loading secondary, same as 'vertex-ai'
Switching between claude-merge and gemini-merge does not duplicate secondary model load
Tests pass

userStories.title: DB migration: auto-migrate gemini-merge → claude-merge
userStories.description: Add gemini-merge → claude-merge to the _DEPRECATED_PP migration mapping in state_db.py. When get_state() reads 'gemini-merge' from DB, it auto-writes back 'claude-merge' (since claude-merge is now the default). This makes the switch automatic for existing users without losing their preference for the merge workflow. Users who explicitly set gemini-fix or haiku-fix are NOT migrated (those are intentional choices, not the default).
acceptanceCriteria: _DEPRECATED_PP dict in state_db.py contains 'gemini-merge': 'claude-merge'
get_state() detects 'gemini-merge' in DB and writes back 'claude-merge'
Migration logs a notice (informational, not error level)
gemini-fix is NOT in _DEPRECATED_PP (users who picked it explicitly stay on it)
haiku-fix is NOT in _DEPRECATED_PP
Tests pass
